title: Researcher-Writer Crew
constraints: The writer must use the researcher's actual output, not generate from scratch
Both agents must remain separate with distinct roles
You may not hard-code research findings into the writer's prompt
The final report must reference specific data points from the research
hints: In CrewAI, set the write_task's context parameter to include the research_task so it receives the output
In LangChain, pass the research chain's output into the writer's prompt template as a variable
Check that the writer's prompt template has a placeholder for the research content

starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

researcher = Agent(
    role="Market Researcher",
    goal="Find current market data and statistics about a given industry",
    backstory="You are a senior market analyst with 10 years of experience.",
    llm=llm,
)

writer = Agent(
    role="Report Writer",
    goal="Draft a professional market report from research data",
    backstory="You are a business writer who turns raw data into executive summaries.",
    llm=llm,
)

research_task = Task(
    description="Research current market trends and key statistics for the {industry} industry",
    expected_output="A detailed list of market trends, statistics, and key players",
    agent=researcher,
)

# BUG: The write_task has no context linking it to research_task
# TODO: Fix context passing so the writer uses the researcher's findings
write_task = Task(
    description="Write a professional market report for the {industry} industry",
    expected_output="A 300-word executive summary with data-backed insights",
    agent=writer,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, writer],
    tasks=[research_task, write_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"industry": "electric vehicles"})
print(result)
starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

research_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a senior market analyst. Find current market data and statistics."),
    ("human", "Research current market trends and key statistics for the {industry} industry"),
])

# BUG: The writer prompt doesn't include a placeholder for research findings
# TODO: Add a variable for research output and pass it through
write_prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a business writer who turns raw data into executive summaries."),
    ("human", "Write a professional 300-word market report for the {industry} industry"),
])

research_chain = research_prompt | llm
write_chain = write_prompt | llm

# Research runs fine
research_result = research_chain.invoke({"industry": "electric vehicles"})
print("Research:", research_result.content)

# Writer ignores research — it generates from scratch
write_result = write_chain.invoke({"industry": "electric vehicles"})
print("Report:", write_result.content)
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

researcher = Agent(
    name="Market Researcher",
    instructions="You are a senior market analyst with 10 years of experience. Find current market data and statistics.",
)

# BUG: The writer has no access to the researcher's output
# TODO: Pass research findings to the writer
writer = Agent(
    name="Report Writer",
    instructions="You are a business writer who turns raw data into executive summaries.",
)

# Research runs fine
research_result = Runner.run_sync(researcher, "Research current market trends and key statistics for the electric vehicles industry")
print("Research:", research_result.final_output)

# Writer ignores research — it generates from scratch
write_result = Runner.run_sync(writer, "Write a professional 300-word market report for the electric vehicles industry")
print("Report:", write_result.final_output)

evaluationCriteria.label: Research context reaches the writer
evaluationCriteria.description: The writer agent receives the researcher's output as input context

evaluationCriteria.label: Report grounded in research
evaluationCriteria.description: The final report references specific data points and findings from the research step

evaluationCriteria.label: Roles remain separate
evaluationCriteria.description: The researcher and writer are distinct agents with different responsibilities

evaluationCriteria.label: No hallucinated statistics
evaluationCriteria.description: The writer does not invent data that was not present in the research output
